data.body:
## Run 23582249759 failure analysis

Timeline:
- 07:17:49 — Run created (workflow_dispatch)
- 07:19:18 — Fetched tickets branch (--depth=2)
- 07:19:21–07:29:31 — bridge-outbound.py ran (~10 min), produced 2 SYNC events
- 07:29:31 — Committed locally, push rejected (non-fast-forward)

Between 07:19 and 07:29, merge-to-main.sh runs pushed new commits to the tickets branch (the 07:27-07:29 burst of 15 dispatches confirms merge activity). The tickets branch advanced while bridge-outbound.py was running.

The concurrency group correctly serialized the next run (23582633068, created 07:29:27) which started at 07:29:41, fetched fresh tickets state, produced 2 SYNC events, and pushed successfully at 07:41:43.

Conclusion: The failure was correct safety behavior. The 2 SYNC events from the failed run were stale (computed against pre-push state). The successful follow-up run re-processed from current state. No data was lost.

Root cause: excessive dispatches from merge-to-main.sh (Root Cause 1). Fix 1 (SHA guard) will eliminate the burst, which eliminates the concurrent push that caused the conflict.
